description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a green fuselage and dual straight wings, each adorned with roundel insignias typical of early 20th-century military aircraft. The nose is painted red and features a wooden propeller, while the undercarriage consists of two main wheels and a tail skid. The tail section is decorated with blue, white, and red stripes, and the vertical stabilizer is conventional in design. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, likely accommodating a single pilot. The overall construction appears to be primarily metal and wood, with fabric-covered wings and control surfaces.
description: A green vintage biplane with dual straight wings, red nose, and open cockpit, featuring roundel insignias and a striped tail.
description_view_0: Front view showing the red nose, wooden propeller, dual green wings with roundels, and the open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the green fuselage, roundel insignias, open cockpit, and the striped tail section with a visible propeller.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section with blue, white, and red stripes, dual wings, and the vertical stabilizer.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the green fuselage, roundel insignias, open cockpit, and the striped tail, mirroring view 1.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing.
